payload.name: Async investigation via Kafka
payload.description: Make triage investigations asynchronous using Kafka and PostgreSQL. Change POST /api/triage/investigate from synchronous blocking to async: return 202 Accepted with investigation ID immediately, publish the request to a triage.requests Kafka topic, a @KafkaListener processes the investigation by running the 5-step loop, stores the TriageReport result in PostgreSQL, and clients poll GET /api/triage/investigations/{id} for status and results.

goal.answer: Make triage investigations asynchronous using Kafka and PostgreSQL. Change POST /api/triage/investigate from synchronous blocking to async: return 202 Accepted with investigation ID immediately, publish the request to a triage.requests Kafka topic, a @KafkaListener processes the investigation by running the 5-step loop, stores the TriageReport result in PostgreSQL, and clients poll GET /api/triage/investigations/{id} for status and results.

payload.plain: Make triage investigations asynchronous using Kafka and PostgreSQL. Change POST /api/triage/investigate from synchronous blocking to async: return 202 Accepted with investigation ID immediately, publish the request to a triage.requests Kafka topic, a @KafkaListener processes the investigation by running the 5-step loop, stores the TriageReport result in PostgreSQL, and clients poll GET /api/triage/investigations/{id} for status and results.

criteria.answer:
spring-kafka added to triage-agent/build.gradle and compiles
InvestigationJob JPA entity and repository exist, backed by PostgreSQL
InvestigationKafkaProducer publishes investigation requests to 'triage.requests' topic
InvestigationKafkaConsumer with @KafkaListener consumes from 'triage.requests', runs the 5-step loop, and persists TriageReport to PostgreSQL with status COMPLETED or FAILED
POST /api/triage/investigate returns HTTP 202 with {investigationId, status, createdAt} immediately (no blocking)
GET /api/triage/investigation/{id} returns status and the TriageReport when completed
JUnit tests verify 202 response and Kafka produce/consume behavior
Smoke test submits investigation, polls to completion, and asserts structured report fields
Full build and docker compose config remain valid after all changes

constraints.answer: Keep all changes within the triage-agent Gradle module, Do not modify Phase 0/1/2/3/4 services or infrastructure beyond adding necessary Kafka config, Follow existing package conventions (com.example.triageagent.*) and Gradle patterns, Preserve existing POST /api/triage/investigate endpoint path; only change response from 200 to 202 and add polling GET endpoint, The 5-step investigation loop must remain functionally identical, only the invocation mechanism changes from sync to async

phaseSnapshots.name: Async Kafka investigation flow
phaseSnapshots.goal: Convert synchronous investigation into an async Kafka-driven flow with database persistence and polling API.
phaseSnapshots.description: A single vertical slice: schema, producer, consumer, controller, tests, smoke. Sequential due to data dependencies.

steps.description: Add spring-kafka to triage-agent/build.gradle. Create InvestigationJob JPA entity (UUID id, String question, Status enum PENDING/RUNNING/COMPLETED/FAILED, Instant createdAt/completedAt, String reportJson) and InvestigationJobRepository. Update application-test.yml to disable Kafka if needed for fast tests.
Create InvestigationKafkaProducer that converts investigation request to a JSON payload and sends it to 'triage.requests' topic using KafkaTemplate<String, String>.

Create InvestigationKafkaConsumer with @KafkaListener(topics='triage.requests', groupId='triage-agent'). On message receipt: load InvestigationJob from DB by ID, update status to RUNNING, call TriageOrchestratorService.investigate(), serialize report via InvestigationMapper, save reportJson back to the job row, and update status to COMPLETED or FAILED.

Update TriageController: POST /api/triage/investigate returns ResponseEntity.status(202).body({investigationId, status, createdAt}). Add GET /api/triage/investigation/{id} returning ResponseEntity.ok({investigationId, question, status, createdAt, completedAt, report}) where report is populated only when status == COMPLETED.

Add JUnit tests: verify POST returns 202 with job metadata; verify GET returns correct status and report for a seeded InvestigationJob; mock KafkaTemplate to assert message is sent to triage.requests. Update existing controller tests to not break.

Update scripts/triage-agent-smoke-test.sh: POST to /api/triage/investigate, extract investigationId, poll GET /api/triage/investigation/{id} every 5s for up to 60s until status is COMPLETED or FAILED, assert report contains .steps and .rootCause.
Run full build: ./gradlew :triage-agent:build and docker compose config to ensure nothing is broken after adding spring-kafka and JPA entities.

assumptions.answer: The user explicitly chose Kafka-based async over DB-only or SSE approaches. Kafka broker is already running in docker-compose (bank.events). PostgreSQL is already running (Axon event store + caller read model).; Investigation consumer stays in the same triage-agent service for simplicity — no new Gradle module needed; Kafka auto-creation is enabled in docker-compose (KAFKA_AUTO_CREATE_TOPICS_ENABLE=true), so explicit topic creation via AdminClient is optional but not required; Jackson ObjectMapper is already on classpath via spring-boot-starter-web; JSON serialization for Kafka payloads uses Jackson; Existing Phase 3/4 implementation (InvestigationStep, InvestigationState, TriageOrchestratorService, TriageReport, InvestigationMapper) is available as-is and will be invoked from the consumer; Spring Kafka version aligned with Spring Boot 3.4 BOM via spring-ai-bom or spring-boot-dependencies
